title: Early Breastfeeding Initiation And Exclusive Breastfeeding Support Coverage Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring early breastfeeding initiation and exclusive breastfeeding support coverage gaps, linking DHS feeding-practice data, health-facility baby-friendly status, and maternity-protection policy data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: WHO/UNICEF estimate that improving breastfeeding could save 820,000 child lives annually — the single most effective child-survival intervention. Yet only 44 percent of newborns are breastfed within the first hour and 48 percent exclusively breastfed for six months. Baby-Friendly Hospital Initiative certification and maternity-leave policies exist but sub-national linkage to actual feeding practices is absent.

success_metrics.target: Every support-coverage estimate links feeding-practice data, facility data, and policy data with methodology.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review for program claims, and replication for quantitative analyses.
